Hey, um, hey, Vin, I just want to ask you a question about communication skills about the pause. They haven't even finished asking the question. Yeah, I'm like, Yeah, yeah, yeah, I know the question. So when when you pause a lot of the times people, and I used to do this all the time because of the same feeling. Now I used to get really nervous and I didn't like the pause. And as a result, I'm not liking the pause. I would just answer people, I'll answer the questions before they even finish their sentence. And then what I've slowly learned from the pros that do this, the pros that do Q&A, the pros that answer questions, they always take three to five seconds after a question to really just think. And then when they respond, the information that they respond with people value it more, because people feel like, Oh, wow, this person really thought through their response before they articulated it. And look, of course, I understand there are times where you need to give people the answer straight away. Like as if you're in a burning building and people are saying, Where's the exit? Let me get out of here. And you're like, that's how you get murdered. I'm pretty sure they'll just push you into the fire. Please don't take three to five second pauses there. Just tell them where the exit is. But you use your own situational awareness. Often what you'll find is when people ask a question and you pause before you answer it, it kind of increases the perceived value of the response.
